setup.title: MetService Weather
setup.description: Configure your MetService NZ weather location.

data_description.name: The prefix for all entities this integration creates — use your location name (e.g. "Napier") so entities read as "Napier Temperature". Add the integration again with a different name to cover more locations.
data_description.location: The MetService town or rural location weather data is fetched for. Choose the one closest to you — around 150 locations are available.
data_description.marine_region: Optional. Select the coastal region nearest to you to enable tide times, boating conditions, and surf sensors. You will choose specific stations for each on the next screen. Choose "None — skip marine data" if you don't need any of these.

advanced_options.name: Advanced
advanced_options.description: Seasonal products (UV, fire danger, clothes drying) pause for part of the year. Turn on the automatic-removal option to remove their sensors while MetService pauses them — they return automatically when data resumes. Leave it off (default) and they stay, reading unknown while paused.

data.auto_hide_seasonal: Automatically remove sensors while MetService publishes no data

locations.title: Marine locations
locations.description: Choose a specific station for each marine feature, or skip those you don't need.

data_description.tide_url: Select a tide station to get next high/low tide times. Choose "None — skip" to omit tide sensors.
data_description.boating_url: Select a location to get boating conditions and forecast. Choose "None — skip" to omit boating sensors.
data_description.surf_url: Select a surf spot to get wave height, swell, rating, and wind conditions. Choose "None — skip" to omit surf sensors.

error.tide_location_not_found: Could not determine the URL for the selected tide station.
error.boating_location_not_found: Could not determine the URL for the selected boating location.
error.surf_location_not_found: Could not determine the URL for the selected surf location.

deprecated_entity.title: Deprecated sensor {entity_id} is still in use
deprecated_entity.description: The {entity_id} sensor keeps its v2026.7.0 behaviour for existing installs, but it is deprecated and superseded by the {replacement_key} sensor. It is still referenced by: {references}. Please update these to use {replacement_key} — {entity_id} is planned for removal in version 2026.9.0.

hidden_deprecated.title: Deprecated sensors hidden automatically
hidden_deprecated.description: {count} deprecated MetService Weather sensor(s) were hidden because nothing currently references them: {entity_ids}. They still work and keep recording history in the background — you can un-hide any of them individually from Settings > Devices & services > Entities. This notice will not reappear once dismissed, unless another sensor is newly hidden.

removed_entity.title: Removed entity {entity_id} is still in use
removed_entity.description: The {entity_id} entity no longer exists in this MetService Weather installation, but it is still referenced by: {references}. Suggested replacement: {replacement}. Please update these automations/scripts — {entity_id} will not come back.

legacy_entry.title: This MetService Weather entry needs reconfiguration
legacy_entry.description: This entry uses {reason}. Only the public API's current set of ~150 supported locations works since v1.0.0. Select **Reconfigure** on this integration entry and choose one of the current locations, or stay on integration version v0.9.19 if you need the previous behaviour.

forecast_attributes_removed.title: Removed forecast_hourly/forecast_daily attributes are still read
forecast_attributes_removed.description: The following automations/scripts still read the forecast_hourly or forecast_daily weather-entity attributes, which were removed: {references}. Forecasts now come from the weather.get_forecasts action (Home Assistant's 2024.4+ direction) — or use the opt-in Rain next 8 hours / Rain next 24 hours sensors as ready-made replacements.

marine_device_move.title: Marine sensors moved to a new device
marine_device_move.description: Tide, boating, and surf entities now live on the {marine_device} device instead of the main location device — entity IDs are unchanged, so entity-based automations keep working. But device-based triggers, conditions, or actions in the following automations/scripts still target the old device and may no longer fire as expected: {references}. Open each one and re-select the entity, or point it at the {marine_device} device.
